sentences.text: Poirot și cu mine îl așteptam la ceai pe vechiul nostru prieten, inspectorul Japp.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро и я ждали к чаю нашего старого друга, инспектора Джеппа.
sentences.original: Poirot and I were expecting our old friend Inspector Japp of Scotland Yard to tea. We were sitting round the tea-table awaiting his arrival. Poirot had just finished carefully straightening the cups and saucers which our landlady was in the habit of throwing, rather than placing, on the table. He had also breathed heavily on the metal teapot, and polished it with a silk handkerchief. The kettle was on the boil, and a small enamel saucepan beside it contained some thick, sweet chocolate which was more to Poirot’s palate than what he described as “your English poison.”

sentences.text: Deodată am auzit o bătaie în ușă și Japp a intrat repede.
sentences.translationRu: Вдруг мы услышали стук в дверь, и Джепп быстро вошёл.
sentences.original: A sharp “rat-tat” sounded below, and a few minutes afterwards Japp entered briskly.  “Hope I’m not late,” he said as he greeted us.

sentences.text: «Am vorbit cu omul care se ocupă de cazul Davenheim», a spus el.
sentences.translationRu: «Я говорил с человеком, который ведёт дело Давенхайма», — сказал он.
sentences.original: “To tell the truth, I was yarning with Miller, the man who’s in charge of the Davenheim case.”

sentences.text: De trei zile, toate ziarele scriau despre acest caz.
sentences.translationRu: Уже три дня все газеты писали об этом деле.
sentences.original: I pricked up my ears. For the last three days the papers had been full of the strange disappearance of Mr. Davenheim,

sentences.text: Domnul Davenheim era un bancher foarte cunoscut și bogat.
sentences.translationRu: Мистер Давенхайм был очень известный и богатый банкир.
sentences.original: senior partner of Davenheim and Salmon, the well-known bankers and financiers.

sentences.text: Sâmbătă el a ieșit din casă și nimeni nu l-a mai văzut de atunci.
sentences.translationRu: В субботу он вышел из дома, и с тех пор его никто не видел.
sentences.original: On Saturday last he had walked out of his house, and had never been seen since. I looked forward to extracting some interesting details from Japp.

sentences.text: «Cred că azi este greu să dispari», am spus eu.
sentences.translationRu: «Думаю, сегодня трудно исчезнуть», — сказал я.
sentences.original: “I should have thought,” I remarked, “that it would be almost impossible for anyone to ‘disappear’ nowadays.”  Poirot moved a plate of bread and butter the eighth of an inch, and said sharply:  “Be exact, my friend. What do you mean by ‘disappear’? To which class of disappearance are you referring?”  “Are disappearances classified and labelled, then?” I laughed.  Japp smiled also. Poirot frowned at us both.

sentences.text: «Există trei feluri de dispariții», a explicat Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Есть три вида исчезновений», — объяснил Пуаро.
sentences.original: “But certainly they are! They fall into three categories:

sentences.text: «Primul: omul pleacă singur. Al doilea: își pierde memoria.»
sentences.translationRu: «Первое: человек уходит сам. Второе: теряет память.»
sentences.original: First, and most common, the voluntary disappearance. Second, the much abused ‘loss of memory’ case—rare, but occasionally genuine.

sentences.text: «Al treilea: o crimă, și cineva ascunde trupul.»
sentences.translationRu: «Третье: убийство, и кто-то прячет тело.»
sentences.original: Third, murder, and a more or less successful disposal of the body. Do you refer to all three as impossible of execution?”  “Very nearly so, I should think. You might lose your own memory, but some one would be sure to recognize you—especially in the case of a well-known man like Davenheim. Then ‘bodies’ can’t be made to vanish into thin air. Sooner or later they turn up, concealed in lonely places, or in trunks. Murder will out. In the same way, the absconding clerk, or the domestic defaulter, is bound to be run down in these days of wireless telegraphy. He can be headed off from foreign countries; ports and railway stations are watched; and, as for concealment in this country, his features and appearance will be known to every one who reads a daily newspaper. He’s up against civilization.”  “Mon ami,” said Poirot, “you make one error. You do not allow for the fact that a man who had decided to make away with another man—or with himself in a figurative sense—might be that rare machine, a man of method. He might bring intelligence, talent, a careful calculation of detail to the task; and then I do not see why he should not be successful in baffling the police force.”  “But not you, I suppose?” said Japp good-humouredly, winking at me. “He couldn’t baffle you, eh, Monsieur Poirot?”  Poirot endeavoured, with a marked lack of success, to look modest. “Me, also! Why not? It is true that I approach such problems with an exact science, a mathematical precision, which seems, alas, only too rare in the new generation of detectives!”  Japp grinned more widely.  “I don’t know,” he said. “Miller, the man who’s on this case, is a smart chap. You may be very sure he won’t overlook a footprint, or a cigar-ash, or a crumb even. He’s got eyes that see everything.”  “So, mon ami,” said Poirot, “has the London sparrow. But all the same, I should not ask the little brown bird to solve the problem of Mr. Davenheim.”  “Come now, monsieur, you’re not going to run down the value of details as clues?”  “By no means. These things are all good in their way. The danger is they may assume undue importance. Most details are insignificant; one or two are vital.

sentences.text: «Important este creierul, celulele cenușii», a spus Poirot, atingând fruntea.
sentences.translationRu: «Важен мозг, серые клетки», — сказал Пуаро, коснувшись лба.
sentences.original: It is the brain, the little grey cells”—he tapped his forehead—“on which one must rely. The senses mislead.

sentences.text: «Adevărul trebuie căutat înăuntru, nu afară.»
sentences.translationRu: «Правду надо искать внутри, а не снаружи.»
sentences.original: One must seek the truth within—not without.”

sentences.text: «Vrei să spui», a întrebat Japp, «că poți rezolva un caz fără să te miști din scaun?»
sentences.translationRu: «Хочешь сказать», — спросил Джепп, «что можешь раскрыть дело, не вставая с кресла?»
sentences.original: “You don’t mean to say, Monsieur Poirot, that you would undertake to solve a case without moving from your chair, do you?”

sentences.text: «Exact asta vreau să spun, dacă îmi spui toate faptele.»
sentences.translationRu: «Именно это я и хочу сказать, если ты расскажешь мне все факты.»
sentences.original: “That is exactly what I do mean—granted the facts were placed before me. I regard myself as a consulting specialist.”

sentences.text: «Pariez cinci lire că nu poți să-mi spui unde este Davenheim, viu sau mort, într-o săptămână», a spus Japp râzând.
sentences.translationRu: «Спорю на пять фунтов, что не можешь сказать мне, где Давенхайм, живой или мёртвый, за неделю», — сказал Джепп, смеясь.
sentences.original: Japp slapped his knee. “Hanged if I don’t take you at your word. Bet you a fiver that you can’t lay your hand—or rather tell me where to lay my hand—on Mr. Davenheim, dead or alive, before a week is out.”

sentences.text: «Bine, accept pariul», a spus Poirot calm. «Acum spune-mi faptele.»
sentences.translationRu: «Хорошо, принимаю спор», — спокойно сказал Пуаро. «Теперь расскажи мне факты.»
sentences.original: Poirot considered. “Eh bien, mon ami, I accept. Le sport, it is the passion of you English. Now—the facts.”

sentences.text: «Sâmbătă, Davenheim a luat trenul spre casa lui de la țară, The Cedars», a început Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «В субботу Давенхайм сел на поезд к своему загородному дому «Сидры», — начал Джепп.
sentences.original: “On Saturday last, as is his usual custom, Mr. Davenheim took the 12.40 train from Victoria to Chingside, where his palatial country place, The Cedars, is situated.

sentences.text: «După prânz, s-a plimbat prin grădină și vorbea normal cu grădinarii.»
sentences.translationRu: «После обеда он погулял по саду и обычно говорил с садовниками.»
sentences.original: After lunch, he strolled round the grounds, and gave various directions to the gardeners. Everybody agrees that his manner was absolutely normal and as usual.

sentences.text: «După ceai, a spus soției lui: Merg în sat să pun niște scrisori.»
sentences.translationRu: «После чая он сказал жене: иду в деревню отправить письма.»
sentences.original: After tea he put his head into his wife’s boudoir, saying that he was going to stroll down to the village and post some letters.

sentences.text: «Aștept un domn, pe nume Lowen.»
sentences.translationRu: «Жду господина по фамилии Лоуэн.»
sentences.original: He added that he was expecting a Mr. Lowen, on business. If he should come before he himself returned, he was to be shown into the study and asked to wait.

sentences.text: «Davenheim a ieșit pe ușa din față și a mers încet pe alee.»
sentences.translationRu: «Давенхайм вышел через парадную дверь и медленно прошёл по аллее.»
sentences.original: Mr. Davenheim then left the house by the front door, passed leisurely down the drive, and out at the gate, and—

sentences.text: «Și nimeni nu l-a mai văzut din acea clipă.»
sentences.translationRu: «И с того момента его никто больше не видел.»
sentences.original: was never seen again. From that hour, he vanished completely.”

sentences.text: «Foarte frumos, o problemă încântătoare», a murmurat Poirot. «Continuă.»
sentences.translationRu: «Очень красиво, прелестная задача», — пробормотал Пуаро. «Продолжай.»
sentences.original: “Pretty—very pretty—altogether a charming little problem,” murmured Poirot. “Proceed, my good friend.”

sentences.text: «Un sfert de oră mai târziu a venit un om înalt și brunet, pe nume Lowen, și a fost dus în birou», a spus Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Через четверть часа пришёл высокий, тёмный человек по фамилии Лоуэн, и его провели в кабинет», — сказал Джепп.
sentences.original: “About a quarter of an hour later a tall, dark man with a thick black moustache rang the front-door bell, and explained that he had an appointment with Mr. Davenheim. He gave the name of Lowen, and in accordance with the banker’s instructions was shown into the study.

sentences.text: «A trecut o oră, dar Davenheim nu a venit, și Lowen a plecat acasă.»
sentences.translationRu: «Прошёл час, но Давенхайм не пришёл, и Лоуэн уехал домой.»
sentences.original: Nearly an hour passed. Mr. Davenheim did not return. Finally Mr. Lowen rang the bell, and explained that he was unable to wait any longer, as he must catch his train back to town. Mrs. Davenheim apologized for her husband’s absence, which seemed unaccountable, as she knew him to have been expecting the visitor. Mr. Lowen reiterated his regrets and took his departure.

sentences.text: «Duminică am chemat poliția, dar nimeni nu înțelegea nimic», a spus Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «В воскресенье вызвали полицию, но никто ничего не понимал», — сказал Джепп.
sentences.original: “Well, as every one knows, Mr. Davenheim did not return. Early on Sunday morning the police were communicated with, but could make neither head nor tail of the matter. Mr. Davenheim seemed literally to have vanished into thin air.

sentences.text: «El nu a fost la poștă, nimeni nu l-a văzut în sat și nu a luat niciun tren.»
sentences.translationRu: «Он не был на почте, никто не видел его в деревне, и он не сел ни на один поезд.»
sentences.original: He had not been to the post office; nor had he been seen passing through the village. At the station they were positive he had not departed by any train. His own motor had not left the garage. If he had hired a car to meet him in some lonely spot, it seems almost certain that by this time, in view of the large reward offered for information, the driver of it would have come forward to tell what he knew. True, there was a small race-meeting at Entfield, five miles away, and if he had walked to that station he might have passed unnoticed in the crowd. But since then his photograph and a full description of him have been circulated in every newspaper, and nobody has been able to give any news of him. We have, of course, received many letters from all over England, but each clue, so far, has ended in disappointment.

sentences.text: «Luni am descoperit ceva important: în birou este un seif, și cineva l-a spart», a continuat Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «В понедельник мы обнаружили нечто важное: в кабинете есть сейф, и кто-то его взломал», — продолжил Джепп.
sentences.original: “On Monday morning a further sensational discovery came to light. Behind a portière in Mr. Davenheim’s study stands a safe, and that safe had been broken into and rifled.

sentences.text: «Dar ferestrele erau închise pe dinăuntru.»
sentences.translationRu: «Но окна были закрыты изнутри.»
sentences.original: The windows were fastened securely on the inside, which seems to put an ordinary burglary out of court, unless, of course, an accomplice within the house fastened them again afterwards. On the other hand, Sunday having intervened, and the household being in a state of chaos, it is likely that the burglary was committed on the Saturday, and remained undetected until Monday.”  “Précisément,” said Poirot dryly. “Well, is he arrested, ce pauvre M. Lowen?”  Japp grinned. “Not yet. But he’s under pretty close supervision.”  Poirot nodded.

sentences.text: «Ce a fost luat din seif?», a întrebat Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Что взяли из сейфа?» — спросил Пуаро.
sentences.original: “What was taken from the safe? Have you any idea?”

sentences.text: «Mulți bani, niște obligațiuni și o avere în bijuterii», a spus Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Много денег, облигации и состояние в драгоценностях», — сказал Джепп.
sentences.original: “We’ve been going into that with the junior partner of the firm and Mrs. Davenheim. Apparently there was a considerable amount in bearer bonds, and a very large sum in notes, owing to some large transaction having been just carried through. There was also a small fortune in jewellery.

sentences.text: «Toate bijuteriile doamnei Davenheim erau în seif.»
sentences.translationRu: «Все драгоценности миссис Давенхайм были в сейфе.»
sentences.original: All Mrs. Davenheim’s jewels were kept in the safe.

sentences.text: «Soțul ei cumpăra bijuterii tot timpul, aproape în fiecare lună.»
sentences.translationRu: «Её муж покупал драгоценности всё время, почти каждый месяц.»
sentences.original: The purchasing of them had become a passion with her husband of late years, and hardly a month passed that he did not make her a present of some rare and costly gem.”

sentences.text: «O pradă bună», a spus Poirot gânditor. «Ce afacere avea Lowen cu Davenheim?»
sentences.translationRu: «Хорошая добыча», — задумчиво сказал Пуаро. «Какое дело было у Лоуэна с Давенхаймом?»
sentences.original: “Altogether a good haul,” said Poirot thoughtfully. “Now, what about Lowen? Is it known what his business was with Davenheim that evening?”

sentences.text: «Cei doi nu se înțelegeau bine; era vorba despre niște acțiuni din America de Sud.»
sentences.translationRu: «Эти двое плохо ладили; речь шла о каких-то акциях из Южной Америки.»
sentences.original: “Well, the two men were apparently not on very good terms. Lowen is a speculator in quite a small way. Nevertheless, he has been able once or twice to score a coup off Davenheim in the market, though it seems they seldom or never actually met. It was a matter concerning some South American shares which led the banker to make his appointment.”

sentences.text: «Davenheim avea afaceri în America de Sud?», a întrebat Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «У Давенхайма были дела в Южной Америке?» — спросил Пуаро.
sentences.original: “Had Davenheim interests in South America, then?”

sentences.text: «Soția lui spune că a fost la Buenos Aires toamna trecută», a răspuns Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Жена говорит, что прошлой осенью он был в Буэнос-Айресе», — ответил Джепп.
sentences.original: “I believe so. Mrs. Davenheim happened to mention that he spent all last autumn in Buenos Ayres.”  “Any trouble in his home life? Were the husband and wife on good terms?”  “I should say his domestic life was quite peaceful and uneventful.

sentences.text: «Doamna Davenheim este o femeie plăcută, dar simplă.»
sentences.translationRu: «Миссис Давенхайм — приятная, но простая женщина.»
sentences.original: Mrs. Davenheim is a pleasant, rather unintelligent woman. Quite a nonentity, I think.”  “Then we must not look for the solution of the mystery there. Had he any enemies?”  “He had plenty of financial rivals, and no doubt there are many people whom he has got the better of who bear him no particular good-will. But there was no one likely to make away with him—and, if they had, where is the body?”  “Exactly. As Hastings says, bodies have a habit of coming to light with fatal persistency.”

sentences.text: «Un grădinar a văzut pe cineva mergând spre grădina de trandafiri, dar era departe și nu știe cine era», a adăugat Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Один садовник видел, как кто-то шёл к розовому саду, но он был далеко и не знает, кто это был», — добавил Джепп.
sentences.original: “By the way, one of the gardeners says he saw a figure going round to the side of the house toward the rose-garden. The long French window of the study opens on to the rose-garden, and Mr. Davenheim frequently entered and left the house that way. But the man was a good way off, at work on some cucumber frames, and cannot even say whether it was the figure of his master or not. Also, he cannot fix the time with any accuracy. It must have been before six, as the gardeners cease work at that time.”  “And Mr. Davenheim left the house?”  “About half-past five or thereabouts.”

sentences.text: «Ce este după grădina de trandafiri?», a întrebat Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Что находится за розовым садом?» — спросил Пуаро.
sentences.original: “What lies beyond the rose-garden?”

sentences.text: «Un lac, cu o casă de bărci», a răspuns Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Озеро, с лодочным домиком», — ответил Джепп.
sentences.original: “A lake.”  “With a boathouse?”  “Yes, a couple of punts are kept there.

sentences.text: «Mâine vom căuta trupul în lac.»
sentences.translationRu: «Завтра мы будем искать тело в озере.»
sentences.original: I suppose you’re thinking of suicide, Monsieur Poirot? Well, I don’t mind telling you that Miller’s going down to-morrow expressly to see that piece of water dragged. That’s the kind of man he is!”

sentences.text: Poirot a luat ziarul cu fotografia lui Davenheim.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро взял газету с фотографией Давенхайма.
sentences.original: Poirot smiled faintly, and turned to me. “Hastings, I pray you, hand me that copy of the Daily Megaphone. If I remember rightly, there is an unusually clear photograph there of the missing man.”  I rose, and found the sheet required. Poirot studied the features attentively.

sentences.text: «Are părul lung, mustață mare și barbă ascuțită», a observat el.
sentences.translationRu: «У него длинные волосы, большие усы и острая борода», — заметил он.
sentences.original: “H’m!” he murmured. “Wears his hair rather long and wavy, full moustache and pointed beard, bushy eyebrows. Eyes dark?”  “Yes.”  “Hair and beard turning grey?”  The detective nodded.

sentences.text: «Ei bine, ce crezi? Este clar, nu?», a întrebat Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Ну, что думаешь? Всё ясно, не так ли?» — спросил Джепп.
sentences.original: “Well, Monsieur Poirot, what have you got to say to it all? Clear as daylight, eh?”

sentences.text: «Dimpotrivă, este foarte neclar, și asta îmi dă mari speranțe», a spus Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Напротив, всё очень неясно, и это даёт мне большие надежды», — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original: “On the contrary, most obscure.”  The Scotland Yard man looked pleased.  “Which gives me great hopes of solving it,” finished Poirot placidly.  “Eh?”

sentences.text: «Când un caz pare prea clar, nu trebuie să-l crezi: cineva l-a făcut așa.»
sentences.translationRu: «Когда дело кажется слишком ясным, ему нельзя верить: кто-то сделал его таким.»
sentences.original: “I find it a good sign when a case is obscure. If a thing is clear as daylight—eh bien, mistrust it! Some one has made it so.”

sentences.text: «Adu-mi orice noutate», a cerut Poirot. «Sigur», a spus Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Приноси мне любые новости», — попросил Пуаро. «Конечно», — сказал Джепп.
sentences.original: Japp shook his head almost pityingly. “Well, each to their fancy. But it’s not a bad thing to see your way clear ahead.”  “I do not see,” murmured Poirot. “I shut my eyes—and think.”  Japp sighed. “Well, you’ve got a clear week to think in.”  “And you will bring me any fresh developments that arise—the result of the labours of the hard-working and lynx-eyed Inspector Miller, for instance?”  “Certainly. That’s in the bargain.”

sentences.text: Japp a plecat, crezând că pariul era prea ușor pentru el.
sentences.translationRu: Джепп ушёл, думая, что спор для него слишком лёгкий.
sentences.original: “Seems a shame, doesn’t it?” said Japp to me as I accompanied him to the door. “Like robbing a child!”  I could not help agreeing with a smile. I was still smiling as I re-entered the room.  “Eh bien!” said Poirot immediately. “You make fun of Papa Poirot, is it not so?” He shook his finger at me. “You do not trust his grey cells? Ah, do not be confused! Let us discuss this little problem—incomplete as yet, I admit, but already showing one or two points of interest.”

sentences.text: «Lacul!», am spus eu. «Și mai mult, casa de bărci!», a spus Poirot, zâmbind.
sentences.translationRu: «Озеро!» — сказал я. «И даже больше, лодочный домик!» — сказал Пуаро, улыбаясь.
sentences.original: “The lake!” I said significantly.  “And even more than the lake, the boathouse!”  I looked sidewise at Poirot. He was smiling in his most inscrutable fashion. I felt that, for the moment, it would be quite useless to question him further.

sentences.text: A doua seară, Japp a venit din nou și avea vești importante.
sentences.translationRu: На следующий вечер Джепп пришёл снова, и у него были важные вести.
sentences.original: We heard nothing of Japp until the following evening, when he walked in about nine o’clock. I saw at once by his expression that he was bursting with news of some kind.  “Eh bien, my friend,” remarked Poirot. “All goes well? But do not tell me that you have discovered the body of Mr. Davenheim in your lake, because I shall not believe you.”

sentences.text: «Nu am găsit trupul, dar am găsit hainele lui în lac», a spus el.
sentences.translationRu: «Мы не нашли тело, но нашли его одежду в озере», — сказал он.
sentences.original: “We haven’t found the body, but we did find his clothes—

sentences.text: «Sunt exact hainele pe care le purta în acea zi.»
sentences.translationRu: «Это точно та одежда, которую он носил в тот день.»
sentences.original: the identical clothes he was wearing that day. What do you say to that?”  “Any other clothes missing from the house?”  “No, his valet is quite positive on that point. The rest of his wardrobe is intact. There’s more.

sentences.text: «Și am arestat pe Lowen», a continuat Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «И мы арестовали Лоуэна», — продолжил Джепп.
sentences.original: We’ve arrested Lowen.

sentences.text: «O slujnică l-a văzut în grădina de trandafiri.»
sentences.translationRu: «Служанка видела его в розовом саду.»
sentences.original: One of the maids, whose business it is to fasten the bedroom windows, declares that she saw Lowen coming towards the study through the rose-garden about a quarter past six. That would be about ten minutes before he left the house.”  “What does he himself say to that?”

sentences.text: «La început el a mințit și a spus că nu a ieșit din birou.»
sentences.translationRu: «Сначала он соврал и сказал, что не выходил из кабинета.»
sentences.original: “Denied first of all that he had ever left the study. But the maid was positive,

sentences.text: «Apoi a spus că a ieșit doar să vadă un trandafir. O poveste cam slabă!»
sentences.translationRu: «Потом сказал, что вышел только посмотреть на розу. Слабоватая история!»
sentences.original: and he pretended afterwards that he had forgotten just stepping out of the window to examine an unusual species of rose. Rather a weak story!

sentences.text: «Mai este o dovadă», a adăugat Japp. «Davenheim purta mereu un inel de aur cu un diamant.»
sentences.translationRu: «Есть ещё одна улика», — добавил Джепп. «Давенхайм всегда носил золотое кольцо с бриллиантом.»
sentences.original: And there’s fresh evidence against him come to light. Mr. Davenheim always wore a thick gold ring set with a solitaire diamond on the little finger of his right hand.

sentences.text: «Acel inel a fost vândut la Londra, sâmbătă noaptea, de un om pe nume Billy Kellett.»
sentences.translationRu: «Это кольцо продал в Лондоне в субботу ночью человек по имени Билли Келлетт.»
sentences.original: Well, that ring was pawned in London on Saturday night by a man called Billy Kellett!

sentences.text: «Poliția îl cunoaște deja: a stat trei luni la închisoare toamna trecută.»
sentences.translationRu: «Полиция уже его знает: прошлой осенью он отсидел три месяца в тюрьме.»
sentences.original: He’s already known to the police—did three months last autumn for lifting an old gentleman’s watch. It seems he tried to pawn the ring at no less than five different places, succeeded at the last one,

sentences.text: «El a băut mult, a lovit un polițist și a fost prins.»
sentences.translationRu: «Он много выпил, ударил полицейского и был пойман.»
sentences.original: got gloriously drunk on the proceeds, assaulted a policeman, and was run in in consequence. I went to Bow Street with Miller and saw him. He’s sober enough now, and I don’t mind admitting we pretty well frightened the life out of him, hinting he might be charged with murder.

sentences.text: «Kellett ne-a spus o poveste ciudată», a continuat Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Келлетт рассказал нам странную историю», — продолжил Джепп.
sentences.original: This is his yarn, and a very queer one it is.

sentences.text: «Sâmbătă, el s-a oprit pe drum să se odihnească și a văzut un domn brunet cu o mustață mare.»
sentences.translationRu: «В субботу он остановился на дороге отдохнуть и увидел тёмного господина с большими усами.»
sentences.original: “He was at Entfield races on Saturday, though I dare say scarfpins was his line of business, rather than betting. Anyway, he had a bad day, and was down on his luck. He was tramping along the road to Chingside, and sat down in a ditch to rest just before he got into the village. A few minutes later he noticed a man coming along the road to the village, ‘dark-complexioned gent, with a big moustache, one of them city toffs,’ is his description of the man.

sentences.text: «Domnul s-a uitat în jur și a aruncat un obiect mic peste gard.»
sentences.translationRu: «Господин осмотрелся и бросил маленький предмет за изгородь.»
sentences.original: “Kellett was half concealed from the road by a heap of stones. Just before he got abreast of him, the man looked quickly up and down the road, and seeing it apparently deserted he took a small object from his pocket and threw it over the hedge. Then he went on towards the station. Now, the object he had thrown over the hedge had fallen with a slight ‘chink’ which aroused the curiosity of the human derelict in the ditch.

sentences.text: «Kellett a căutat și a găsit inelul!»
sentences.translationRu: «Келлетт поискал и нашёл кольцо!»
sentences.original: He investigated and, after a short search, discovered the ring! That is Kellett’s story. It’s only fair to say that Lowen denies it utterly, and of course the word of a man like Kellett can’t be relied upon in the slightest.

sentences.text: «Poate Kellett l-a omorât pe Davenheim», a spus Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Может, Келлетт убил Давенхайма», — сказал Джепп.
sentences.original: It’s within the bounds of possibility that he met Davenheim in the lane and robbed and murdered him.”

sentences.text: Dar Poirot a clătinat din cap. «Foarte puțin probabil.»
sentences.translationRu: Но Пуаро покачал головой. «Очень маловероятно.»
sentences.original: Poirot shook his head.  “Very improbable, mon ami. He had no means of disposing of the body. It would have been found by now. Secondly, the open way in which he pawned the ring makes it unlikely that he did murder to get it.

sentences.text: «Un hoț mic ca el nu este de obicei un ucigaș.»
sentences.translationRu: «Мелкий вор, как он, обычно не убийца.»
sentences.original: Thirdly, your sneak-thief is rarely a murderer.

sentences.text: «Și el este în închisoare de sâmbătă.»
sentences.translationRu: «И он в тюрьме с субботы.»
sentences.original: Fourthly, as he has been in prison since Saturday, it would be too much of a coincidence that he is able to give so accurate a description of Lowen.”  Japp nodded. “I don’t say you’re not right. But all the same, you won’t get a jury to take much note of a jailbird’s evidence. What seems odd to me is that Lowen couldn’t find a cleverer way of disposing of the ring.”  Poirot shrugged his shoulders. “Well, after all, if it were found in the neighbourhood, it might be argued that Davenheim himself had dropped it.”  “But why remove it from the body at all?” I cried.

sentences.text: «Lângă lac este un cuptor de var», a spus Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Возле озера есть печь для извести», — сказал Джепп.
sentences.original: “There might be a reason for that,” said Japp. “Do you know that just beyond the lake, a little gate leads out on to the hill, and not three minutes’ walk brings you to—what do you think?—a lime kiln.”

sentences.text: «Varul poate distruge un trup, dar nu poate distruge inelul de metal. Ce crimă oribilă!», am spus eu.
sentences.translationRu: «Известь может уничтожить тело, но не металлическое кольцо. Какое ужасное преступление!» — сказал я.
sentences.original: “Good heavens!” I cried. “You mean that the lime which destroyed the body would be powerless to affect the metal of the ring?”  “Exactly.”  “It seems to me,” I said, “that that explains everything. What a horrible crime!”

sentences.text: Toți l-am privit pe Poirot. El gândea adânc, apoi a pus o întrebare foarte ciudată:
sentences.translationRu: Все мы посмотрели на Пуаро. Он глубоко думал, потом задал очень странный вопрос:
sentences.original: By common consent we both turned and looked at Poirot. He seemed lost in reflection, his brow knitted, as though with some supreme mental effort. I felt that at last his keen intellect was asserting itself. What would his first words be? We were not long left in doubt. With a sigh, the tension of his attitude relaxed, and turning to Japp, he asked:

sentences.text: «Domnul și doamna Davenheim dormeau în aceeași cameră?»
sentences.translationRu: «Мистер и миссис Давенхайм спали в одной комнате?»
sentences.original: “Have you any idea, my friend, whether Mr. and Mrs. Davenheim occupied the same bedroom?”

sentences.text: Întrebarea părea fără sens, și Japp a râs.
sentences.translationRu: Вопрос казался бессмысленным, и Джепп рассмеялся.
sentences.original: The question seemed so ludicrously inappropriate that for a moment we both stared in silence. Then Japp burst into a laugh. “Good Lord, Monsieur Poirot, I thought you were coming out with something startling. As to your question, I’m sure I don’t know.”

sentences.text: «Nu știu, dar pot să aflu, dacă chiar vrei», a spus el.
sentences.translationRu: «Не знаю, но могу узнать, если ты правда хочешь», — сказал он.
sentences.original: “You could find out?” asked Poirot with curious persistence.  “Oh, certainly—if you really want to know.”  “Merci, mon ami. I should be obliged if you would make a point of it.”

sentences.text: Japp a plecat, crezând că Poirot greșea.
sentences.translationRu: Джепп ушёл, думая, что Пуаро ошибается.
sentences.original: Japp stared at him a few minutes longer, but Poirot seemed to have forgotten us both. The detective shook his head sadly at me, and murmuring, “Poor old fellow! War’s been too much for him!” gently withdrew from the room.  As Poirot still seemed sunk in a daydream, I took a sheet of paper, and amused myself by scribbling notes upon it. My friend’s voice aroused me. He had come out of his reverie, and was looking brisk and alert.  “Que faites-vous là, mon ami?”

sentences.text: Eu am scris pe o hârtie patru idei despre caz.
sentences.translationRu: Я написал на бумаге четыре мысли о деле.
sentences.original: “I was jotting down what occurred to me as the main points of interest in this affair.”  “You become methodical—at last!” said Poirot approvingly.  I concealed my pleasure. “Shall I read them to you?”  “By all means.”  I cleared my throat.

sentences.text: «Unu: toate dovezile arată că Lowen a spart seiful. Doi: el era supărat pe Davenheim.»
sentences.translationRu: «Первое: все улики говорят, что сейф взломал Лоуэн. Второе: он был зол на Давенхайма.»
sentences.original: “‘One: All the evidence points to Lowen having been the man who forced the safe.  “‘Two: He had a grudge against Davenheim.

sentences.text: «Trei: el a mințit. Patru: povestea lui Kellett îl acuză pe Lowen.»
sentences.translationRu: «Третье: он соврал. Четвёртое: история Келлетта обвиняет Лоуэна.»
sentences.original: “‘Three: He lied in his first statement that he had never left the study.  “‘Four: If you accept Billy Kellett’s story as true, Lowen is unmistakably implicated.’”  I paused. “Well?” I asked, for I felt that I had put my finger on all the vital facts.

sentences.text: Poirot s-a uitat la mine cu milă. «Tu uiți lucrurile importante.»
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро посмотрел на меня с жалостью. «Ты забываешь важное.»
sentences.original: Poirot looked at me pityingly, shaking his head very gently. “Mon pauvre ami! But it is that you have not the gift! The important detail, you appreciate him never! Also, your reasoning is false.”  “How?”  “Let me take your four points.

sentences.text: «Lowen nu putea ști că va fi singur în birou.»
sentences.translationRu: «Лоуэн не мог знать, что окажется один в кабинете.»
sentences.original: “One: Mr. Lowen could not possibly know that he would have the chance to open the safe. He came for a business interview. He could not know beforehand that Mr. Davenheim would be absent posting a letter, and that he would consequently be alone in the study!”  “He might have seized his opportunity,” I suggested.

sentences.text: «Și un domn nu poartă unelte pentru spart seifuri.»
sentences.translationRu: «И господин не носит с собой инструменты для взлома сейфов.»
sentences.original: “And the tools? City gentlemen do not carry round housebreaker’s tools on the off chance! And one could not cut into that safe with a penknife, bien entendu!”

sentences.text: «Tu ai uitat două lucruri foarte importante», a spus Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Ты забыл две очень важные вещи», — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original: “Well, what about Number Two?”  “You say Lowen had a grudge against Mr. Davenheim. What you mean is that he had once or twice got the better of him. And presumably those transactions were entered into with the view of benefiting himself. In any case you do not as a rule bear a grudge against a man you have got the better of—it is more likely to be the other way about. Whatever grudge there might have been would have been on Mr. Davenheim’s side.”  “Well, you can’t deny that he lied about never having left the study?”  “No. But he may have been frightened. Remember, the missing man’s clothes had just been discovered in the lake. Of course, as usual, he would have done better to speak the truth.”  “And the fourth point?”  “I grant you that. If Kellett’s story is true, Lowen is undeniably implicated. That is what makes the affair so very interesting.”  “Then I did appreciate one vital fact?”  “Perhaps—but you have entirely overlooked the two most important points, the ones which undoubtedly hold the clue to the whole matter.”  “And pray, what are they?”

sentences.text: «Unu: Davenheim cumpăra mereu bijuterii. Doi: călătoria lui la Buenos Aires.»
sentences.translationRu: «Первое: Давенхайм всё время покупал драгоценности. Второе: его поездка в Буэнос-Айрес.»
sentences.original: “One, the passion which has grown upon Mr. Davenheim in the last few years for buying jewellery. Two, his trip to Buenos Ayres last autumn.”

sentences.text: «Poirot, glumești!» «Sunt foarte serios», a răspuns el.
sentences.translationRu: «Пуаро, ты шутишь!» «Я совершенно серьёзен», — ответил он.
sentences.original: “Poirot, you are joking!”  “I am most serious. Ah, sacred thunder, but I hope Japp will not forget my little commission.”

sentences.text: A doua zi a venit o telegramă de la Japp. Eu am citit-o cu voce tare:
sentences.translationRu: На следующий день пришла телеграмма от Джеппа. Я прочитал её вслух:
sentences.original: But the detective, entering into the spirit of the joke, had remembered it so well that a telegram was handed to Poirot about eleven o’clock the next day. At his request I opened it and read it out:

sentences.text: «Soțul și soția dorm în camere separate din iarna trecută.»
sentences.translationRu: «Муж и жена спят в отдельных комнатах с прошлой зимы.»
sentences.original: “‘Husband and wife have occupied separate rooms since last winter.’”

sentences.text: «Aha! Acum este iunie! Totul este rezolvat!», a strigat Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Ага! А сейчас июнь! Всё раскрыто!» — воскликнул Пуаро.
sentences.original: “Aha!” cried Poirot. “And now we are in mid June! All is solved!”  I stared at him.  “You have no moneys in the bank of Davenheim and Salmon, mon ami?”  “No,” I said, wondering. “Why?”  “Because I should advise you to withdraw it—before it is too late.”  “Why, what do you expect?”

sentences.text: «Banca Davenheim va cădea în câteva zile», a spus Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Банк Давенхайма рухнет через несколько дней», — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original: “I expect a big smash in a few days—perhaps sooner. Which reminds me, we will return the compliment of a dépêche to Japp. A pencil, I pray you, and a form. Voilà! ‘Advise you to withdraw any money deposited with firm in question.’ That will intrigue him, the good Japp! His eyes will open wide—wide! He will not comprehend in the slightest—until to-morrow, or the next day!”  I remained sceptical, but the morrow forced me to render tribute to my friend’s remarkable powers.

sentences.text: A doua zi, în toate ziarele scria despre căderea băncii Davenheim.
sentences.translationRu: На следующий день во всех газетах писали о крахе банка Давенхайма.
sentences.original: In every paper was a huge headline telling of the sensational failure of the Davenheim bank. The disappearance of the famous financier took on a totally different aspect in the light of the revelation of the financial affairs of the bank.

sentences.text: Japp a intrat repede. «Cum ai știut?», a strigat el.
sentences.translationRu: Джепп быстро вошёл. «Как ты узнал?» — воскликнул он.
sentences.original: Before we were half-way through breakfast, the door flew open and Japp rushed in. In his left hand was a paper; in his right was Poirot’s telegram, which he banged down on the table in front of my friend.  “How did you know, Monsieur Poirot? How the blazes could you know?”

sentences.text: «De la început, seiful spart mi s-a părut ciudat», a spus Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «С самого начала взломанный сейф показался мне странным», — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original: Poirot smiled placidly at him. “Ah, mon ami, after your wire, it was a certainty! From the commencement, see you, it struck me that the safe burglary was somewhat remarkable.

sentences.text: «Bijuterii, bani, obligațiuni — totul pregătit pentru cine? Pentru Davenheim însuși!»
sentences.translationRu: «Драгоценности, деньги, облигации — всё приготовлено для кого? Для самого Давенхайма!»
sentences.original: Jewels, ready money, bearer bonds—all so conveniently arranged for—whom? Well, the good Monsieur Davenheim was of those who ‘look after Number One’ as your saying goes! It seemed almost certain that it was arranged for—himself!

sentences.text: «El a furat banii băncii, i-a schimbat în bijuterii și poate a pus bijuterii false în loc.»
sentences.translationRu: «Он украл деньги банка, превратил их в драгоценности и, может, поставил вместо них фальшивые.»
sentences.original: Then his passion of late years for buying jewellery! How simple! The funds he embezzled, he converted into jewels, very likely replacing them in turn with paste duplicates,

sentences.text: «Și a ascuns o avere sub alt nume.»
sentences.translationRu: «И спрятал состояние под другим именем.»
sentences.original: and so he put away in a safe place, under another name, a considerable fortune to be enjoyed all in good time when every one has been thrown off the track.

sentences.text: «Apoi a chemat pe Lowen, un dușman vechi, a făcut singur o gaură în seif și a ieșit din casă.»
sentences.translationRu: «Потом он позвал Лоуэна, старого врага, сам проделал дыру в сейфе и вышел из дома.»
sentences.original: His arrangements completed, he makes an appointment with Mr. Lowen (who has been imprudent enough in the past to cross the great man once or twice), drills a hole in the safe, leaves orders that the guest is to be shown into the study, and walks out of the house—where?”

sentences.text: «Unde s-a dus?», a întrebat Japp. «La ascunzătoarea lui», a spus Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Куда он ушёл?» — спросил Джепп. «В своё убежище», — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original: Poirot stopped, and stretched out his hand for another boiled egg. He frowned. “It is really insupportable,” he murmured, “that every hen lays an egg of a different size! What symmetry can there be on the breakfast table? At least they should sort them in dozens at the shop!”  “Never mind the eggs,” said Japp impatiently. “Let ’em lay ’em square if they like. Tell us where our customer went to when he left The Cedars—that is, if you know!”  “Eh bien, he went to his hiding-place. Ah, this Monsieur Davenheim, there may be some malformation in his grey cells, but they are of the first quality!”  “Do you know where he is hiding?”  “Certainly! It is most ingenious.”  “For the Lord’s sake, tell us, then!”  Poirot gently collected every fragment of shell from his plate, placed them in the egg-cup, and reversed the empty egg-shell on top of them. This little operation concluded, he smiled on the neat effect, and then beamed affectionately on us both.

sentences.text: «Gândiți-vă: dacă ați fi acest om, unde v-ați ascunde?», a întrebat Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Подумайте: если бы вы были этим человеком, где бы вы спрятались?» — спросил Пуаро.
sentences.original: “Come, my friends, you are men of intelligence. Ask yourselves the question which I asked myself. ‘If I were this man, where should I hide?’ Hastings, what do you say?”

sentences.text: «Eu aș rămâne la Londra, printre mulți oameni», am spus eu. «Eu aș fugi imediat cu o navă, departe», a spus Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Я остался бы в Лондоне, среди многих людей», — сказал я. «Я сразу бежал бы на корабле, далеко», — сказал Джепп.
sentences.original: “Well,” I said, “I’m rather inclined to think I’d not do a bolt at all. I’d stay in London—in the heart of things, travel by tubes and buses; ten to one I’d never be recognized. There’s safety in a crowd.”  Poirot turned inquiringly to Japp.  “I don’t agree. Get clear away at once—that’s the only chance. I would have had plenty of time to prepare things beforehand. I’d have a yacht waiting, with steam up, and I’d be off to one of the most out-of-the-way corners of the world before the hue and cry began!”

sentences.text: Poirot a tăcut un moment, apoi a zâmbit ciudat.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро на миг замолчал, потом странно улыбнулся.
sentences.original: We both looked at Poirot. “What do you say, monsieur?”  For a moment he remained silent. Then a very curious smile flitted across his face.

sentences.text: «Prieteni, dacă eu m-aș ascunde de poliție, știți unde m-aș ascunde? Într-o închisoare!»
sentences.translationRu: «Друзья, если бы я прятался от полиции, знаете, где бы я спрятался? В тюрьме!»
sentences.original: “My friends, if I were hiding from the police, do you know where I should hide? In a prison!”  “What?”

sentences.text: «Voi îl căutați ca să-l puneți în închisoare, deci nu vă uitați dacă este deja acolo!»
sentences.translationRu: «Вы ищете его, чтобы посадить в тюрьму, поэтому не смотрите, нет ли его уже там!»
sentences.original: “You are seeking Monsieur Davenheim in order to put him in prison, so you never dream of looking to see if he may not be already there!”  “What do you mean?”

sentences.text: «Davenheim este omul Billy Kellett!», a spus Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Давенхайм — это человек Билли Келлетт!» — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original: “You tell me Madame Davenheim is not a very intelligent woman. Nevertheless I think that if you took her to Bow Street and confronted her with the man Billy Kellett, she would recognize him!

sentences.text: «El și-a ras barba, mustața și sprâncenele, și și-a tăiat părul scurt.»
sentences.translationRu: «Он сбрил бороду, усы и брови и подстриг волосы коротко.»
sentences.original: In spite of the fact that he has shaved his beard and moustache and those bushy eyebrows, and has cropped his hair close.

sentences.text: «Dar dacă soția lui îl vede, îl va recunoaște.»
sentences.translationRu: «Но если жена его увидит, она узнает его.»
sentences.original: A woman nearly always knows her husband, though the rest of the world may be deceived!”

sentences.text: «Dar Kellett este cunoscut de poliție!», a strigat Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Но Келлетта знает полиция!» — воскликнул Джепп.
sentences.original: “Billy Kellett? But he’s known to the police!”

sentences.text: «Davenheim nu a fost la Buenos Aires toamna trecută», a răspuns Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Давенхайм не был в Буэнос-Айресе прошлой осенью», — ответил Пуаро.
sentences.original: “Did I not tell you Davenheim was a clever man? He prepared his alibi long beforehand. He was not in Buenos Ayres last autumn—

sentences.text: «În acel timp, el a creat personajul Billy Kellett.»
sentences.translationRu: «В то время он создавал персонажа Билли Келлетта.»
sentences.original: he was creating the character of Billy Kellett,

sentences.text: «A stat trei luni la închisoare special, ca poliția să nu bănuiască nimic.»
sentences.translationRu: «Он специально отсидел три месяца в тюрьме, чтобы полиция ничего не заподозрила.»
sentences.original: ‘doing three months,’ so that the police should have no suspicions when the time came. He was playing, remember, for a large fortune, as well as liberty. It was worth while doing the thing thoroughly. Only——”  “Yes?”

sentences.text: «Dar era o problemă: ca să fie din nou Davenheim, el trebuia să poarte o barbă falsă», a continuat Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Но была одна проблема: чтобы снова быть Давенхаймом, ему приходилось носить фальшивую бороду», — продолжил Пуаро.
sentences.original: “Eh bien, afterwards he had to wear a false beard and wig, had to make up as himself again,

sentences.text: «Dar nu poți dormi cu o barbă falsă, deci dormea separat de soția lui.»
sentences.translationRu: «Но нельзя спать с фальшивой бородой, поэтому он спал отдельно от жены.»
sentences.original: and to sleep with a false beard is not easy—it invites detection! He cannot risk continuing to share the chamber of madame his wife. You found out for me that for the last six months, or ever since his supposed return from Buenos Ayres, he and Mrs. Davenheim occupied separate rooms.

sentences.text: «Când am aflat asta, am fost sigur. Totul se potrivea.»
sentences.translationRu: «Когда я узнал это, я был уверен. Всё сходилось.»
sentences.original: Then I was sure! Everything fitted in. The gardener who fancied he saw his master going round to the side of the house was quite right.

sentences.text: «Davenheim a mers la casa de bărci, a pus hainele de vagabond și a aruncat hainele bune în lac», a spus Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Давенхайм пошёл к лодочному домику, надел одежду бродяги и бросил хорошую одежду в озеро», — сказал Пуаро.
sentences.original: He went to the boathouse, donned his ‘tramp’ clothes, which you may be sure had been safely hidden from the eyes of his valet, dropped the others in the lake,

sentences.text: «Apoi a vândut inelul în mod vizibil și a lovit un polițist.»
sentences.translationRu: «Потом он заметно продал кольцо и ударил полицейского.»
sentences.original: and proceeded to carry out his plan by pawning the ring in an obvious manner, and then assaulting a policeman,

sentences.text: «Așa a intrat în siguranță în închisoare, unde nimeni nu îl caută!»
sentences.translationRu: «Так он в безопасности попал в тюрьму, где никто его не ищет!»
sentences.original: getting himself safely into the haven of Bow Street, where nobody would ever dream of looking for him!”

sentences.text: «Este imposibil», a murmurat Japp.
sentences.translationRu: «Это невозможно», — пробормотал Джепп.
sentences.original: “It’s impossible,” murmured Japp.

sentences.text: «Întreab-o pe doamna Davenheim», a spus prietenul meu, zâmbind.
sentences.translationRu: «Спроси миссис Давенхайм», — сказал мой друг, улыбаясь.
sentences.original: “Ask Madame,” said my friend, smiling.

sentences.text: A doua zi, o scrisoare era lângă farfuria lui Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: На следующий день возле тарелки Пуаро лежало письмо.
sentences.original: The next day a registered letter lay beside Poirot’s plate.

sentences.text: El a deschis-o și o bancnotă de cinci lire a căzut din ea: Japp pierduse pariul.
sentences.translationRu: Он открыл его, и из него выпала банкнота в пять фунтов: Джепп проиграл спор.
sentences.original: He opened it, and a five-pound note fluttered out. My friend’s brow puckered.

sentences.text: «Sărmanul Japp! Am o idee: cinăm împreună, noi trei!», a spus Poirot, zâmbind.
sentences.translationRu: «Бедный Джепп! У меня идея: поужинаем вместе, мы трое!» — сказал Пуаро, улыбаясь.
sentences.original: “Ah, sacré! But what shall I do with it? I have much remorse! Ce pauvre Japp! Ah, an idea! We will have a little dinner, we three! That consoles me. It was really too easy. I am ashamed. I, who would not rob a child—mille tonnerres! Mon ami, what have you, that you laugh so heartily?”     X
